questions.question: What is the primary message of the poem 'Try Again'?
questions.correct_answer: Perseverance and persistence lead to success
questions.explanation: The poet emphasizes that failure is not a reason to quit, but a reason to work harder.

questions.question: According to the poet, what should you do if you don't succeed at first?
questions.explanation: The core advice is to repeat the effort until the goal is achieved.

questions.question: Why is failure not considered a disgrace in the poem?
questions.correct_answer: Because it shows that you are making an effort
questions.explanation: The poet suggests that trying and failing is more honorable than not trying at all.

questions.question: What quality does the poet say is necessary to win a race?
questions.explanation: Steadfastness allows a person to overcome obstacles and eventually succeed.

questions.question: What should one do if the task is hard?
questions.correct_answer: Keep working because time will bring the reward
questions.explanation: Difficulty is not an excuse to stop; patience and time help solve hard problems.

questions.question: What example does the poet use to show that success is possible?
questions.correct_answer: Other people who have succeeded through effort
questions.explanation: If others can do it, there is no reason why the reader cannot achieve the same with patience.

questions.question: What does 'keep this rule in view' mean?
questions.correct_answer: Always remember the advice to try again
questions.explanation: It serves as a constant reminder to stay motivated during tough times.

questions.question: How should we react to the 'shame' of losing?
questions.correct_answer: By ignoring the shame and focusing on the next attempt
questions.explanation: The poem encourages us to look past temporary setbacks to reach the final goal.

questions.question: What is the reward for trying again?
questions.correct_answer: The ultimate prize of success and self-respect
questions.explanation: Hard work eventually pays off, granting the person what they were striving for.

questions.question: Who is the author of the poem 'Try Again'?
questions.explanation: W.E. Hickson was a British educational writer who popularized this motivational poem.

notes.title: Universal Rule
notes.content: The poem presents 'Try Again' as a universal rule for all situations in life.

notes.title: Positive Outlook
notes.content: The poet maintains an optimistic tone, viewing failure as a stepping stone rather than a wall.

notes.title: Patience and Time
notes.content: Success is often a matter of waiting and working consistently over a period of time.

notes.title: Overcoming Difficulty
notes.content: If a task is difficult, it simply means it requires more effort and focus.

notes.title: Self-Improvement
notes.content: The poem encourages personal growth by challenging the reader to never give up.

facts.fact: The poem uses a simple AABB or ABAB rhyme scheme to make the lesson memorable.
The phrase 'Try, try again' has become one of the most famous proverbs in the English language.
Perseverance is defined in the poem as the ability to keep going even after a setback.
